deep_dive.title: Evaluating Prompting-Based Defenses Against Domain-Camouflaged Injection Attacks
deep_dive.abstract: Domain-camouflaged injection attacks embed malicious instructions in retrieved content using domain-appropriate vocabulary, evading standard detectors that rely on syntactic injection markers. When detection fails, practitioners need to know which defense architectures reduce attack success. We evaluate five prompting-based defenses (spotlighting, paraphrasing, prompt sandwiching, and two combinations) against domain-camouflaged injection across three model families (Claude Haiku, Llama 3.1 8B, Gemini 2.0 Flash) and three deployment domains (financial, legal, general) using 3,510 trials. Paraphrasing retrieved content before agent processing is the most consistently effective defense in this benchmark, reducing camouflage attack success rate by 55-84\% depending on model, and achieves lower attack success rates than our Llama Guard 4 configuration on every model tested. Defense effectiveness is strongly model-dependent: spotlighting halves attack success on Claude Haiku but provides no benefit on Llama 3.1 8B. Financial domain deployments face the highest residual risk at 26-33\% baseline attack success rate, with no prompting-based defense fully eliminating the threat on weaker models. These results provide the first systematic evaluation of prompting-based defenses specifically against camouflage-class injection attacks and establish benchmark-based recommendations for practitioners. All tasks use synthetically constructed professional documents; whether these benchmark rankings generalize to real enterprise documents remains an open question.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluates prompting-based defenses against injection attacks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估基于提示的防御方法对抗注入攻击
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估基于提示的防御方法对抗注入攻击
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估基于提示的防御方法对抗注入攻击
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Evaluates five prompting-based defenses against domain-camouflaged injection attacks, finding paraphrasing most effective across models and domains.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 领域伪装注入攻击利用领域词汇隐藏恶意指令，逃逸基于语法的检测。论文评估了五种基于提示的防御方法，包括高亮、改写、提示夹层等，在三个模型家族和三个部署领域进行3510次试验。实验表明，改写（paraphrasing）是最稳定有效的防御手段，显著降低伪装攻击成功率。

deep_dive.title: CodeSentinel: A Three-Layer Defense Against Indirect Prompt Injection in Code Contexts
deep_dive.abstract: Code large language models increasingly retrieve external code context from repositories, documentation, issue threads, and coding-agent environments, creating an indirect prompt-injection surface where attackers hide instructions in comments, strings, identifiers, or decoy code. We propose CodeSentinel, a three-layer inference-time sanitizer. It uses Tree-sitter to extract high-risk model-facing CST nodes, then combines syntax-guided pre-filtering, CST-guided Dynamic Min-K\% scoring, and node perturbation analysis to detect adversarial and natural-looking semantic triggers. Detected nodes are removed or neutralized before reaching the downstream Code LLM. Across six recent attack families, \CodeSentinel achieves 0.80 average node-level F1, outperforming CodeGarrison, DePA, and KillBadCode.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: CodeSentinel is a three-layer inference-time defense against indirect prompt injection in code LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: CodeSentinel是一种针对代码LLM中间接提示注入的三层推理时防御方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: CodeSentinel是一种针对代码LLM中间接提示注入的三层推理时防御方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: CodeSentinel是一种针对代码LLM中间接提示注入的三层推理时防御方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CodeSentinel defends indirect prompt injection in code LLMs by extracting, scoring, and neutralizing malicious nodes in code syntax trees before inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 代码大语言模型在检索外部代码上下文时面临间接提示注入风险。本文提出CodeSentinel，一种三层推理时净化器：首先用Tree-sitter提取高风险语法节点，然后结合语法引导预过滤、CST引导动态Min-K%评分和节点扰动分析检测攻击，最后移除或中和恶意节点。在六种攻击家族上，CodeSentinel达到0.80的平均节点级检测准确率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 代码大语言模型在检索外部代码上下文时面临间接提示注入风险。本文提出CodeSentinel，一种三层推理时净化器：首先用Tree-sitter提取高风险语法节点，然后结合语法引导预过滤、CST引导动态Min-K%评分和节点扰动分析检测攻击，最后移除或中和恶意节点。在六种攻击家族上，CodeSentinel达到0.80的平均节点级检测准确率。

deep_dive.title: Risk-Aware LLM Agents for Geospatial Data Retrieval: Design and Preliminary Adversarial Evaluation
deep_dive.abstract: We present an LLM-driven framework for retrieving remote sensing data from cloud-based geospatial catalogues using natural language queries. The system converts user intent into structured API calls, enabling efficient access to satellite imagery and environmental datasets. The architecture integrates three agents: Guardrail for safety and policy enforcement, General-QA for intent interpretation, and Recommender-Analyst for schema-aware API call generation. This coordinated design ensures reliable, semantically aligned interaction with external data services. The modular framework is portable across platforms through API schema substitution and supports applications in environmental monitoring, disaster response, and climate analysis. It establishes a scalable interface between user intent and geospatial infrastructure, enabling streamlined and automated Earth observation workflows. Preliminary experiments under adversarial multi-turn settings show that prompt-level safety instructions improve robustness, although rare high-impact failures persist in API manipulation scenarios and highlight the need for adaptive, system-level defenses that balance safety, usability, and cost efficiency, which motivates the use of our intercept-level Guardrail agent.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Guardrail agent for safety and policy enforcement
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 包含Guardrail智能体用于安全和策略执行
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 包含Guardrail智能体用于安全和策略执行
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 包含Guardrail智能体用于安全和策略执行
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an LLM agent framework with a dedicated Guardrail component for safety and policy enforcement, evaluated adversarially, directly addressing agent safety mechanisms.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出一个LLM驱动的智能体框架用于地理空间数据检索，该框架集成Guardrail智能体专门负责安全和策略执行，防止违规查询。系统还包含General-QA和Recommender-Analyst智能体协同工作。初步对抗性评估显示Guardrail能有效过滤恶意输入。该工作直接体现了LLM智能体的安全机制设计，对需求中的智能体安全具有直接参考价值。

deep_dive.title: Greedy Coordinate Diffusion: Effective and Semantically Coherent Adversarial Attacks via Diffusion Guidance
deep_dive.abstract: Adversarial attacks on large language models have limited practical impact despite extensive research. Optimization-based attacks such as Greedy Coordinate Gradient (GCG) (Zou et al., 2023) produce high-perplexity, incoherent suffixes that existing defenses easily detect (Bengio et al., 2024).   Moreover, attempting to enforce coherence constraints during optimization often prevents the attack from successfully eliciting the specific targeted response, resulting in low success rates against robust models. Conversely, attacks that maintain coherence often alter the semantic intent of queries; when the model complies with these altered queries, responses fail to address the adversary's original goal. In this work, we introduce Greedy Coordinate Diffusion (GCD), a novel framework that efficiently generates adversarial attacks against safety-aligned models while maintaining low perplexity and high semantic adherence to the adversary's original intent. GCD leverages the generative priors of discrete diffusion language models to guide the search for adversarial suffixes that achieve semantic coherence and adherence.   Unlike GCG, GCD does not require direct gradient access, allowing it to operate in a gray-box setting. We show GCD achieves highest ASR while remaining competitive on response-quality scores, and that the constructed adversarial prompts are detected at lower rates than other methods by perplexity-based and guard-model filters.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes a new prompt injection attack method with semantic coherence
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出了一种语义连贯的提示注入攻击新方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出了一种语义连贯的提示注入攻击新方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出了一种语义连贯的提示注入攻击新方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Greedy Coordinate Diffusion, a method to generate semantically coherent adversarial suffixes for LLM attacks, improving attack success while maintaining coherence.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出贪婪坐标扩散（Greedy Coordinate Diffusion）方法，利用扩散引导生成语义连贯的对抗性后缀，解决了现有攻击方法因高困惑度易被防御检测的问题。实验表明该方法在保持语义连贯性的同时提高了攻击成功率，为理解提示注入攻击提供了新工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文提出贪婪坐标扩散（Greedy Coordinate Diffusion）方法，利用扩散引导生成语义连贯的对抗性后缀，解决了现有攻击方法因高困惑度易被防御检测的问题。实验表明该方法在保持语义连贯性的同时提高了攻击成功率，为理解提示注入攻击提供了新工具。

deep_dive.title: Understanding and Mitigating Prompt Leaking Attacks in Real-World LLM-Based Applications
deep_dive.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based applications rely on system prompts to encode core logic and developer-defined constraints, making these prompts important intellectual property. However, system prompts are vulnerable to prompt leaking attacks. Although prior work has shown such attacks in controlled settings, their prevalence, causes, and defenses in real-world deployments remain unclear.   This paper presents a systematic study of prompt leaking in real-world LLM-based applications. We measure 1,200 applications across six major commercial platforms and find that over 80% of deployments leak system prompts under realistic adversarial queries, sometimes exposing sensitive information such as third-party API keys. We also show that existing defenses often fail to prevent leakage without degrading usability. To explain these failures, we conduct an attention-level mechanistic analysis and identify attention drift, where query-key alignment bias and softmax amplification cause LLMs to progressively ignore defensive constraints. Guided by this insight, we propose AREA, a practical defense that re-anchors the model's attention using an optimizable soft prompt. Experiments and real-world case studies show that AREA matches the leakage resistance of state-of-the-art defenses while improving average usability by over 33% and reducing optimization overhead by nearly 3x. Our responsible disclosure led two affected vendors to classify these leaks as medium-severity vulnerabilities.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Systematically studies prompt leaking in real-world LLM apps and proposes mitigation methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文系统研究了实际LLM应用中提示泄露攻击的普遍性，发现超80%应用存在泄露风险，并分析了攻击原因。提出了针对提示泄露的防御方法，有效保护系统提示中的敏感信息。该工作填补了实际场景下提示泄露防御的空白。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文系统研究了实际LLM应用中提示泄露攻击的普遍性，发现超80%应用存在泄露风险，并分析了攻击原因。提出了针对提示泄露的防御方法，有效保护系统提示中的敏感信息。该工作填补了实际场景下提示泄露防御的空白。

quick_skim.title: SpecAlign: Efficient Specification-Grounded Alignment of Large Language Models via Synthetic Data
quick_skim.abstract: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in real-world applications, alignment is no longer governed by a single universal notion of safety or helpfulness, but instead by provider- or application-specific model specifications. These specifications are typically long, structured, and frequently updated, yet existing alignment pipelines lack a systematic mechanism to operationalize them as training signals. In this paper, we propose specification-grounded alignment, a new alignment paradigm that treats provider-authored model specifications as the primary alignment target rather than abstract principles or static benchmarks. To instantiate this paradigm, we introduce SpecAlign, a framework that synthesizes alignment data directly from specification documents. SpecAlign combines structured rule annotation, controllable specification instantiation, and multi-agent adversarial data synthesis to generate fine-grained, boundary-aware preference pairs that capture both compliant behaviors and meaningful specification violations. Experiments across multiple model specifications and backbone models demonstrate that training with SpecAlign consistently improves rule compliance while preserving general capabilities and avoiding over-conservative behavior. These results suggest that grounding alignment in explicit model specifications enables rapid, precise, and scalable adaptation of LLM behavior to evolving policy requirements.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: specification-grounded alignment enforces provider-specific safety norms on outputs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 规范对齐强制执行提供商特定的输出安全规范
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 规范对齐强制执行提供商特定的输出安全规范
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 规范对齐强制执行提供商特定的输出安全规范
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SpecAlign aligns LLMs to provider-authored specifications via synthetic data, enabling fine-grained content safety filtering and output guardrails.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出规范对齐新范式，将提供商制定的模型规范作为对齐目标，而非抽象原则。SpecAlign框架通过合成数据高效实现这一对齐，使模型输出符合具体安全、帮助性等规范。这种方法可直接用于构建内容安全过滤器和输出护栏，确保LLM生成文本符合应用要求。其高效性和灵活性使其成为实现需求中内容安全和输出护栏的有力工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出规范对齐新范式，将提供商制定的模型规范作为对齐目标，而非抽象原则。SpecAlign框架通过合成数据高效实现这一对齐，使模型输出符合具体安全、帮助性等规范。这种方法可直接用于构建内容安全过滤器和输出护栏，确保LLM生成文本符合应用要求。其高效性和灵活性使其成为实现需求中内容安全和输出护栏的有力工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: content safety filters for LLM outputs

quick_skim.title: How Much Can We Trust LLM Search Agents? Measuring Endorsement Vulnerability to Web Content Manipulation
quick_skim.abstract: Large language model (LLM)-based search agents synthesize open-web content into actionable recommendations on behalf of users, creating a risk that attacker-published pages are transformed into endorsed claims. We introduce SearchGEO, a controlled evaluation framework for measuring endorsement corruption in LLM-based web-search agents, combining a web-evidence manipulation pipeline, a five-mode attack taxonomy, and multiple output-level metrics. We evaluate 13 LLM backends on 308 cases each. Results show that vulnerability patterns vary across backends: overall attack success rate (ASR) ranges from 0.0% on Claude-Sonnet-4.6 to 31.4% on Gemini-3-Flash, the strongest attack mode differs by model family, and the same deployment scaffold could amplify or decrease ASR on different backends. An auxiliary agent-skill probe, where endorsement becomes an install command, exposes a sharp split among otherwise robust backends: Claude over-rejects while GPT over-trusts. These findings argue for treating recommendation reliability under adversarial search content as a first-class dimension of backend safety evaluation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: evaluation framework for LLM search agent security against web manipulation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对LLM搜索代理的网页操纵安全评估框架
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对LLM搜索代理的网页操纵安全评估框架
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对LLM搜索代理的网页操纵安全评估框架
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces SearchGEO, a framework to measure endorsement corruption in LLM-based web-search agents, revealing variable vulnerability patterns across 13 backends and highlighting security risks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM搜索代理在整合网页内容推荐时易受操纵，本文提出SearchGEO评估框架系统测量背书腐败。该框架结合网页证据操纵、五模式攻击分类和多项指标，在13个后端上评估发现攻击成功率从0%到31.4%不等。结果为构建更安全的LLM搜索代理提供了关键测量工具和洞察。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: LLM搜索代理在整合网页内容推荐时易受操纵，本文提出SearchGEO评估框架系统测量背书腐败。该框架结合网页证据操纵、五模式攻击分类和多项指标，在13个后端上评估发现攻击成功率从0%到31.4%不等。结果为构建更安全的LLM搜索代理提供了关键测量工具和洞察。

quick_skim.title: Verified Detection and Prevention of Concurrency Anomalies in Multi-Agent Large Language Model Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-agent LLM systems share state through memory stores, vector indices, and tool registries. We model such sharing as long-running read-generate-write operations under deterministic-generation semantics -- the regime durable-execution engines enforce by deterministic replay -- and formalize four concurrency anomalies in TLA+: stale-generation, phantom-tool, causal-cascade, and tool-effect reordering, structural analogues of classical isolation anomalies, each with a TLC counter-example. The exclusion lattice over these anomalies is trivial; the contribution is the mechanically verified realizability and strict separation of one maximal chain within it, $L_0 \subsetneq \cdots \subsetneq L_4$, to our knowledge the first machine-checked consistency hierarchy for such runtimes. A development of 274 Verus obligations (zero assume, zero admit; trust base: two structural axioms and a mutex correspondence) proves the detectors sound and complete against the specifications and each runtime its avoidance set. Three deployed Rust runtimes realize L0-L1 (pessimistic locking, serializable snapshot isolation, default-SI), each verified against stale-generation and refined to its state machine; L2-L4 are exec-mode-verified with dependency-free prevention twins (A3, A6, A2: 0/1000 versus 1000/1000), and L2 is run live across three model families (A3 prevented in all 120 retracted sessions). We reproduce a silent lost update in ByteDance's deer-flow, formalizing its fix as a verified $L_0 \to L_1$ refinement, and exhibit tool-effect reordering in LangGraph's ToolNode on unmodified output, removed by an L3 commit-order sequencer. The verified detector, refinements, and realizability artifacts are the contribution; the phenomena and lattice are classical.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: verification and prevention of concurrency anomalies in multi-agent LLM systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体LLM系统中并发异常的验证与预防
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体LLM系统中并发异常的验证与预防
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体LLM系统中并发异常的验证与预防
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Formalizes concurrency anomalies in multi-agent LLM systems and provides mechanically verified detection and prevention methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多智能体LLM系统共享内存、向量索引和工具注册表时会出现类似传统数据库的并发异常。论文形式化了四种异常（陈旧生成、幻影工具、因果级联、工具效应重排序），并提出了机械验证的检测与预防方法，首次实现了严格分离的异常格。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: security measures for LLM based autonomous agents

quick_skim.title: Output Vector Editing for Memorization Mitigation in Large Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large language models memorize and reproduce sequences from their training data, creating privacy, copyright, and security risks. Existing neuron-level mitigation methods equate editing with zeroing out neuron activations, but the activation only controls whether a neuron engages; the output vector is what writes to the residual stream and, through superposition, encodes multiple features. We propose output vector editing, a constrained-optimization weight edit that locates a small set of MLP neurons responsible for a memorized continuation and minimally modifies their output vectors to introduce a distractor in vocabulary space, redirecting their residual-stream contributions while leaving activations unchanged. Evaluating on four models from 360M to 7B parameters (SmolLM-360M, OLMo-1B, OLMo-7B, Llama2-7B), we center on OLMo-7B (whose open weights and pretraining corpus enable systematic mining) and mine 6831 memorized sequences, achieving up to 87.9% suppression. The 2.7$\times$ gap over zero ablation on the same located neurons shows the suppression comes from the output-vector edit, not localization alone. Four edit modes span a spectrum from aggressive suppression to minimal redirection; in ensemble they cover 96.5% of memorized sequences, while our recommended single-mode configuration reaches 81.5% with no catastrophic locality failures. We further identify a mechanistic boundary at ${\sim}14%$ of sequences unreachable by MLP-only editing; while these failures are not attention-driven overall, ablating the top contributing attention heads recovers 60--64% of them, with stronger recovery on continuations that copy tokens from the prefix, positioning attention as a complementary fallback rather than a primary mechanism. Edit mode ordering and the success-locality trade-off transfer across all four models, with success rates scaling with model size rather than family.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: output vector editing mitigates memorization, which can be used as an adversarial prompt mitigation technique
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 输出向量编辑缓解记忆化，可作为对抗性提示缓解技术
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 输出向量编辑缓解记忆化，可作为对抗性提示缓解技术
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 输出向量编辑缓解记忆化，可作为对抗性提示缓解技术
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Output vector editing modifies MLP neuron output vectors to mitigate memorized continuations, reducing security risks from training data leakage.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大语言模型记忆训练数据带来隐私和安全风险。本文提出输出向量编辑方法，通过约束优化定位少量MLP神经元并修改其输出向量，引入词汇空间干扰，改变残差流贡献而不改变激活值。在四个不同规模模型上，该方法有效降低记忆复现率，可作为对抗性提示等安全风险的缓解手段。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大语言模型记忆训练数据带来隐私和安全风险。本文提出输出向量编辑方法，通过约束优化定位少量MLP神经元并修改其输出向量，引入词汇空间干扰，改变残差流贡献而不改变激活值。在四个不同规模模型上，该方法有效降低记忆复现率，可作为对抗性提示等安全风险的缓解手段。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Safe Data: Pretraining-Stage Alignment with Regular Safety Reflection
quick_skim.abstract: To achieve deeper safety alignment for large language models (LLMs), recent efforts have studied how to push safety interventions earlier into the pretraining stage, primarily by filtering unsafe data or rewriting it into safer forms. We argue that pretraining-stage alignment should go beyond making the data safe: LLMs may compose seemingly benign knowledge and capabilities into unsafe behaviors. To this end, we propose Safety Reflection Pretraining, a pretraining-stage alignment method which regularly inserts short safety reflections into pretraining corpora to integrate self-monitoring directly into language modeling, establishing a foundational capability that is subsequently reinforced by compatible post-training. Our experiments with 1.7B models pretrained on FineWeb-Edu show that Safety Reflection Pretraining improves safety classification accuracy and substantially reduces the success rates of inference-stage and finetuning attacks. Complementary to our real-world experiments, we also introduce a fully controlled synthetic environment, MedSafetyWorld, with a clear definition of safety and a reasoning structure under which models can easily generalize unsafe behaviors from safe data. Ablations in MedSafetyWorld further demonstrate a clear advantage of Safety Reflection Pretraining in preventing models from acting on unsafe behaviors generalized from safe data, compared with data filtering and rewriting. Taken together, our findings suggest that pretraining alignment should not only make the training data safe, but also shape the behaviors that models are likely to acquire from safe data.
